task_completion.description: Create the root node of the attack tree
task_completion.visual_evidence: A node at the top or center of the diagram explicitly labeled 'LockBit Ransomware Infection'.

task_completion.description: Build a hierarchical tree with Tactics and Techniques
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiple nodes branching out from the root node, connected by lines/edges, representing Tactics (Level 1) and specific Techniques (Levels 2/3).

task_completion.description: Include explicit MITRE T-codes in the diagram
task_completion.visual_evidence: Nodes contain specific MITRE IDs such as T1566, T1003, T1021, T1486, T1490, etc.

task_completion.description: Use AND/OR logic gates to represent relationships
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels, distinct shapes, or explicit annotations showing 'AND' or 'OR' between sibling nodes or branches.

task_completion.description: Apply specific color coding to the nodes based on their category
task_completion.visual_evidence: The root node and Impact nodes are colored Red, Lateral Movement/Escalation nodes are Orange, and Initial Access/Reconnaissance nodes are Blue.

task_completion.description: Save as .drawio and export as .png to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: Save/Export dialogs in draw.io showing 'lockbit_attack_tree.drawio' and 'lockbit_attack_tree.png' being saved to the Desktop, or file manager/desktop icons showing these files exist.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io Desktop application GUI to create the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the draw.io application interface open with the diagram being actively constructed, rather than just a terminal window generating an XML file.

integrity.description: The agent opened and read the provided lockbit_report.txt file
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal (cat/less), or file viewer displaying the contents of '~/Desktop/lockbit_report.txt'.

integrity.description: The diagram was built incrementally rather than loaded from a pre-existing completed file
integrity.visual_evidence: Sequential screenshots show nodes, edges, and colors being added step-by-step over time.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating an attack tree for LockBit 3.0 based on CISA AA23-075A. Key MITRE codes to look for in the diagram include: T1566 (Phishing), T1003 (Credential Dumping/Mimikatz), T1021 (Remote Services/RDP/SMB), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery/VSS deletion), T1569.002 (PsExec), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1059 (Command and Scripting), and T1053 (Scheduled Tasks). The diagram must have at least 8 nodes and 7 edges. Color requirements: Red for Root Goal and Impact (e.g., T1486, T1490), Orange for Lateral Movement/Escalation (e.g., T1021, T1569.002), and Blue for Initial Access/Reconnaissance (e.g., T1566).
